FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Jul-25-2022 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Jun-23-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Feder, via Zoom, for Jewish Family and Children's Services, Trustee.

The court advises of its tentative ruling to grant the petitions as prayed except as to the discharge of bond. Mr. Feder offers advocacy regarding the bond issue and of the plan to obtain non-court supervised bonds. The court advises that it will consider his testimony and will make changes to the proposed order if needed.

The petition is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Martinez. Not Reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-17-301095 Conservatorship of George J. Duranto,
PTR-19-302850 Geroge J. Duranto Charitable Remainder Trust and PTR-19-302851 George J. Duranto 2019 Revocable Living Trust.

Appearances: Bruce Feder appeared by zoom for Jewish Family & Children's Services ("JFCS") (Conservator/Trustee).
Rebeca Paul of JFCS appeared by zoom.

The Court stated that it had the chance to go through all of the accountings and the petitions, noted that there was a lot of work involved in all three cases, that there is a request for fees that goes beyond the accounting period and stated that its overall concern is that when totaled, fees are at about 3.5%. The Court also stated that it is wondering whether there would be any benefit in having the same individuals on this case to get a better handle on not duplicating services and reducing fees. Mr. Feder stated that JFCS structures its services by having a Care Manager who deals with the person, an Account Manager who deals with financial issues and Ms. Paul who supervises everybody, reviews the reports and reviews the pleadings. Mr. Feder also stated that the functions are bifurcated, that he believes there is not any duplicating, that Mr. Duranto is 96 years old, that he was able to benefit from the charitable remainder trust and liquidity of the home, and that Mr. Duranto saved two to three hundred thousand dollars in capital gains taxes. In addition, Mr. Feder stated that JFCS keeps pretty detailed time logs, that they need to be redacted to make sure no confidential information is available to the public, that there could be over a thousand entries, and that Ms. Paul takes a lot of time looking over the entries to make sure there are no duplications and that no privileged information is available to the public. Furthermore, Mr. Feder stated that a lot of time is spent in responding to the Probate Examiner's notes and looking over the fee logs to make sure the entries are in the correct case, and that reducing the bond would require filing of an ex parte petition and that it would save $73.00 in premiums. The Second Account and Report of Conservator and Petition for Settlement, etc., the Petitions to Settle First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. (2) are under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
